title: PAR(2) Discovery Engine: Circadian Clock-Target Dynamics Analysis Platform
description: A statistical platform for analyzing circadian clock-target gene dynamics using AR(2) autoregressive modeling. The eigenvalue modulus |lambda| quantifies temporal persistence in gene expression across 79 embedded datasets spanning 5 species (Mus musculus, Homo sapiens, Papio anubis, Arabidopsis thaliana, Saccharomyces cerevisiae) and ~125,000 unique genes. Features: 6-check edge-case diagnostics, multi-tissue Phase Portrait Explorer with BMAL1 coupling analysis (12 tissues, 636 gene-tissue tests), root-space geometry with GO/KEGG enrichment, drug target overlay (168 genes, 256 FDA drugs), literature validation against 59 curated circadian genes, genome-wide coupling scan with BH-FDR correction, ODE Model Zoo (5 models, 12/12 checks PASS), framework benchmarks against ARIMA/OU/State-Space, 11-analysis robustness suite, and custom CSV upload for real-time analysis.
notes: DUAL LICENSE: Free for academic/research use; commercial license required for pharmaceutical, biotech, or revenue-generating applications. Contact mickwh@msn.com for commercial licensing. PATENT NOTICE: The PAR(2) methodology is subject to a pending UK patent application. Platform scale: ~130,000 lines of code, 253 files, 48 frontend routes, 79 embedded datasets spanning 5 species. Key validation results: ODE Model Zoo 12/12 predictions PASS, multi-species hierarchy preserved across 8 core validated datasets (4 species), 6-check edge-case diagnostics screening every AR(2) result, literature validation against 59 curated circadian genes with 180x enrichment over controls.
references: Stokes K, et al. (2021) The circadian clock gene BMAL1 coordinates intestinal regeneration. Genes Dev. 35(17-18):1290-1301.
Hughes ME, et al. (2009) Harmonics of circadian gene transcription in mammals. PLoS Genet 5(4):e1000442.
Zhang R, et al. (2014) A circadian gene expression atlas in mammals. Proc Natl Acad Sci 111(45):16219-16224.
Mure LS, et al. (2018) Diurnal transcriptome atlas of a primate across major neural and peripheral tissues. Science 359(6381).
Redmond S, et al. (2024) Circadian clock gene expression in Arabidopsis thaliana developmental stages. GSE242964.
